name: Cameroon

outbound_official.note: no official Cameroon government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Cameroon Ministry of External Relations (MINREX, diplocam.cm) and related official domains (passcam.cm, dgsn.cm, evisacam.cm, prc.cm) do not publish an outbound list of visa-free/visa-on-arrival/e-visa destinations for Cameroon ordinary-passport holders. All official Cameroon government consular pages focus exclusively on inbound visa services for foreigners visiting Cameroon. The MINREX Twitter handle (@diplocam_minrex) and Facebook (minrex.cm) were also checked - no such list was published there either. No analog to India's mea.gov.in/vffin page was found for Cameroon.

visa_free.notes: CEMAC member. Ordinary passport holders can enter Cameroon visa-free under the CEMAC Free Movement Agreement effective since Equatorial Guinea's ratification in 2017. Biometric passport required. Confirmed via cameroontradehub.cm (official Cameroon Trade Hub, citing Cameroon Tribune N 11461 of 27 October 2017) and Wikipedia Visa policy of Cameroon. The Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt page (ambacamcaire.com) does not list Equatorial Guinea explicitly among exempt nations for ordinary passports; Embassy Japan also does not list GNQ for ordinary passports. However, CEMAC free movement treaty is official government-confirmed via cameroontradehub.cm. Entry retained per safety rule and CEMAC treaty confirmation. See gaps.

CEMAC member. Ordinary passport holders can enter Cameroon visa-free under the CEMAC Free Movement Agreement; Gabon ratified in October 2017. Biometric passport required. Confirmed via cameroontradehub.cm (official Cameroon Trade Hub) and Wikipedia Visa policy of Cameroon. The Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt page (ambacamcaire.com) does not list Gabon explicitly among exempt nations for ordinary passports. CEMAC free movement treaty is official government-confirmed via cameroontradehub.cm. Entry retained per CEMAC treaty confirmation. See gaps.

CEMAC member. Nationals explicitly exempt from visa requirement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page (ambacamcaire.com) and via cameroontradehub.cm CEMAC free movement article (Congo ratified October 23, 2017). Biometric passport required under CEMAC agreement.

CEMAC member. Nationals explicitly exempt from visa requirement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page (ambacamcaire.com). Biometric passport required under CEMAC agreement.

CEMAC member. Nationals explicitly exempt from visa requirement. Confirmed on Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page (ambacamcaire.com). Biometric passport required under CEMAC agreement.

Free movement for all nationals - need only present a valid official document. Bilateral agreement. Explicitly confirmed on both the Official Embassy of Cameroon in Japan visa page (cameroonemb-jp.org) and the Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt visa exemption page (ambacamcaire.com): 'Nationals of Nigeria … are exempt from the visa requirement to enter Cameroon.'

Ordinary passport holders can enter Cameroon under the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons and Goods. Embassy Japan (cameroonemb-jp.org) describes this as an 'expense-free (gratis) visa' for ordinary passport holders. Wikipedia notes Mali's exemption was cancelled in September 2015 then later restored at an unspecified date. The Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt page does NOT list Mali among visa-exempt nations. Classification retained as visa_free consistent with Embassy Japan confirmation and prior validation. See gaps for unresolved discrepancy.

e_visa.nationality: All other nationalities (ordinary passport holders not covered by exemption agreements)
e_visa.notes: All ordinary passport holders not covered by bilateral exemption agreements must apply online through the official evisacam.cm portal (operated by MINREX). Short-stay visa up to 6 months (180 days) - normal processing 72 hours, fee XAF 100,000 / approx. EUR 152; express processing 24 hours, fee XAF 150,000 / approx. EUR 229. Long-stay visa up to 12 months (360 days) - fee XAF 150,000 normal / XAF 200,000 express per diplocam.cm. Transit visa up to 5 days (free) per diplocam.cm. NOTE: Cameroon High Commission UK (cameroonhighcommission.co.uk) cites a paid transit visa at EUR 153 for 4-30 days - this may represent a separate paid transit category; the free 5-day transit is sourced from diplocam.cm (MINREX official). Visa sticker affixed at embassy/high commission or at border entry point. Launched April 30, 2023 per Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023 implementing Law No. 97/012 of 10 January 1997.

visa_policy.notes: Cameroon operates a universal e-visa policy via evisacam.cm (MINREX) effective April 30, 2023 under Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023. All ordinary passport holders must apply online unless covered by a bilateral exemption agreement. Seven countries qualify for visa-free ordinary passport entry: all 5 other CEMAC members (Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Republic of Congo, Chad, Central African Republic - biometric passport required under CEMAC) plus Nigeria and Mali (bilateral free movement agreements). Diplomatic and Service/Official passport holders have additional bilateral visa-waiver agreements in force with: Equatorial Guinea, Egypt, Mali, Brazil, Vietnam, Turkey, China, UAE, Switzerland, Italy, Russia, Tunisia, Algeria (ratified January 2026 via Decree No. 2026/034), Republic of Korea (ratified May 2025 via Decree No. 2025/176). Yellow fever vaccination certificate required for all entrants.

conditional_access.conditions: Equatorial Guinea - bilateral visa exemption agreement in force (CEMAC member); exact date not published on checked official sources. Ordinary passport holders separately covered by CEMAC free movement (biometric passport required).

Egypt - bilateral visa exemption agreement in force for diplomatic and service passport holders. Ratification date reported as December 29, 2023 by non-official sources; official decree text on prc.cm not directly verified.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders in force; ordinary passport holders separately covered by Free Movement of Persons and Goods agreement (visa-free).
Bilateral visa exemption agreement; ratification date not directly verified on official prc.cm sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders. Ratified by President Biya on January 29, 2026 per Decree No. 2026/034, confirmed via prc.cm.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; exact date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force for diplomatic passports only (not service passports); 30 days. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 90 days within any 6-month period. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 30 days. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force for diplomatic passports only (not service passports); 30 days. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; exact duration and date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 90 days within any 6-month period. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.
Bilateral visa exemption agreement in force; 90 days within any 180-day period. Exact agreement date not published on checked official sources.

Bilateral visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders. Agreement signed June 2024; RATIFIED May 2, 2025 per Decree No. 2025/176 of 2 May 2025 confirmed on prc.cm. Now in force. Max stay 90 days per Wikipedia Visa policy of Cameroon (prc.cm decree page does not expose full text). Note: Wikipedia (re-fetched 2026-06-29) still shows Korea as 'awaiting ratification' - this appears to be an outdated Wikipedia entry; prc.cm is the authoritative official source confirming ratification.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa (Ordinary)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary visits for tourism, family visits, or other short-term purposes. Covers stays up to 6 months.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via evisacam.cm portal. Fee XAF 100,000 / approx. EUR 152 (diplocam.cm confirmed 2026-06-29). Multiple entry available. Passport must be valid at least 6 months. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required. Biometric procedures completed at embassy or at Cameroon border entry point.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Express Visa
visa_types.purpose: Expedited temporary entry visa for tourism or short-term visits with 24-hour processing.
visa_types.notes: Express processing option available through evisacam.cm portal. Fee XAF 150,000 / approx. EUR 229 (diplocam.cm confirmed 2026-06-29). Same eligibility and documentation as ordinary short-stay visa. Multiple entry available.

visa_types.name: Short-Stay Visa (Business)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term entry for business meetings, conferences, trade negotiations, or commercial activities up to 6 months.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via evisacam.cm portal. The short-stay visa category covers business purposes. Fee XAF 100,000 / approx. EUR 152 per diplocam.cm. Express option at XAF 150,000 / approx. EUR 229 / 24 hours available.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa (Contract Worker / Independent Worker)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals intending to reside and work in Cameroon under employment contract or as independent workers, valid 6-12 months.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via evisacam.cm portal (MINREX). Fee XAF 150,000 normal / XAF 200,000 express per diplocam.cm (confirmed 2026-06-29). Cameroon High Commission UK cites EUR 305 for long-stay normal which does not match diplocam.cm; diplocam.cm is the MINREX primary source. Categories include: contract workers, independent workers, and long-term trainees. Issued under Law No. 97/012 of 10 January 1997 and Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa (Student)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled in Cameroon educational institutions for studies longer than 6 months.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via evisacam.cm portal (MINREX). Fee XAF 150,000 normal per diplocam.cm (confirmed 2026-06-29). Long-term trainees and students are listed as a category under the long-stay visa scheme per Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023.

visa_types.name: Long-Stay Visa (Family Reunification)
visa_types.purpose: For family members of foreign nationals lawfully residing in Cameroon who wish to join them for an extended period.
visa_types.notes: Applied online via evisacam.cm portal (MINREX). Family members of aliens staying in Cameroon are an explicit long-stay visa sub-category per Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023. Fee XAF 150,000 normal per diplocam.cm (confirmed 2026-06-29).

visa_types.name: Transit Visa (up to 5 days, free)
visa_types.purpose: For travellers transiting through Cameroon for up to 5 days en route to another destination.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Applied online via evisacam.cm. Diplocam.cm (MINREX official, re-confirmed 2026-06-29) specifies 5-day maximum for the free transit visa. Cameroon High Commission UK (cameroonhighcommission.co.uk) separately lists a paid transit visa at EUR 153 for 4-30 days - this appears to be a different (paid) transit product or an inconsistency; diplocam.cm (MINREX) is authoritative for the free 5-day transit. Passport must be valid at least 6 months.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Courtesy / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for holders of diplomatic, service, or official passports on official duty from countries that do not have a bilateral visa waiver agreement with Cameroon.
visa_types.notes: No visa fees charged for diplomatic, service, and special passports on official duty. Bilateral visa waiver agreements exist (full exemption) for: Equatorial Guinea, Egypt, Mali, Brazil, Vietnam, Turkey, China, UAE, Switzerland, Italy, Russia, Tunisia, Algeria (Decree No. 2026/034, Jan 2026), Republic of Korea (Decree No. 2025/176, May 2025). Countries not covered by such agreements still require a visa but pay no fee.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship-by-Investment program found for Cameroon. No official government source publishes any such program. Aggregators confirm the absence of a CBI program.

programs.name: Long-Stay Visa (Aliens - investors/workers)
programs.notes: Cameroon issues long-stay visas (up to 12 months) under Law No. 97/012 of 10 January 1997 and Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023 for aliens wishing to reside or work in Cameroon. Categories include: contract workers, independent workers, long-term trainees, students, and family members of aliens staying in Cameroon. Employment contracts must be signed by the Minister in charge of employment. No specific investor minimum investment amount is published on official sources. No formal 'golden visa' or investor-specific residence program with defined thresholds exists on official government websites.

programs.name: Short-Stay Express Visa (evisacam.cm)
programs.eligibility: All ordinary passport holders eligible to apply for a Cameroon visa (same eligibility as standard short-stay visa)
programs.notes: Express processing option available through the official evisacam.cm portal (MINREX) at an additional fee. Standard short-stay visa costs XAF 100,000 / approx. EUR 152; express short-stay costs XAF 150,000 / approx. EUR 229 (diplocam.cm confirmed 2026-06-29). Long-stay normal XAF 150,000; long-stay express XAF 200,000 per diplocam.cm. Standard processing is 72 hours (3 working days). Referenced in Decree No. 2023/147 of 02 March 2023.

agreements: CEMAC free movement (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) - 6 member states: Cameroon, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Republic of Congo, Chad, Central African Republic. Effective since all members ratified: Congo and Gabon (October 2017), Equatorial Guinea (completing ratification per cameroontradehub.cm). Biometric passport required.

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 - CONFIRMED CEMAC free movement effective including Equatorial Guinea and Gabon: cameroontradehub.cm (official Cameroon Trade Hub, citing Cameroon Tribune N 11461 of 27 October 2017) confirms the CEMAC Free Movement Agreement has been fully in effect since all 6 members ratified. Biometric passport requirement added to all CEMAC entries. GNQ and GAB source_url updated to cameroontradehub.cm (official government source) from Wikipedia.

2026-06-29 - CONFIRMED diplocam.cm fees (re-fetched): Short-stay ordinary XAF 100,000 / ~EUR 152; short-stay express XAF 150,000 / ~EUR 229; long-stay ordinary XAF 150,000 / ~EUR 229; long-stay express XAF 200,000 / ~EUR 305; transit 5 days free. Fees consistent with prior record; minor EUR rounding note (prior cited EUR 153, diplocam.cm shows ~EUR 152).

2026-06-29 - NOTED Wikipedia inconsistency on Korea (KOR): Wikipedia re-fetched shows Korea as 'awaiting ratification' despite prc.cm Decree No. 2025/176 of May 2, 2025 confirming ratification. prc.cm is authoritative; Wikipedia entry appears outdated. KOR conditional_access entry notes added.

2026-06-29 - CONFIRMED Algeria (DZA) Decree No. 2026/034 of 29 January 2026 via prc.cm (re-fetched): decree title confirms 'Agreement between Cameroon and Algeria on mutual visa exemption for holders of diplomatic and service passports'. Source URL retained (prc.cm page confirmed).

2026-06-29 - NOTED transit visa discrepancy: diplocam.cm says free transit up to 5 days; Cameroon High Commission UK says EUR 153 for transit 4-30 days. These may be different products (free short transit vs. paid longer transit). Free 5-day from diplocam.cm (MINREX primary source) retained. Added to transit visa notes.

2026-06-29 - NOTED Mali status: Wikipedia says Mali's exemption was cancelled September 2015 then later restored at unspecified date. Embassy Japan (cameroonemb-jp.org) confirms current expense-free ordinary visa for Mali. Embassy Egypt (ambacamcaire.com) does NOT list Mali. Mali visa_free entry retained based on Embassy Japan official confirmation; added to gaps.

2026-06-29 - NO CHANGE to visa_free list composition: All 7 entries (GNQ, GAB, COG, CAF, TCD, NGA, MLI) retained with updated source details and CEMAC biometric passport notes. No entries added or removed this pass.

2026-06-27 - ADDED Republic of Korea (KOR) to conditional_access diplomatic/service passport exemptions: Decree No. 2025/176 of 2 May 2025 confirmed on prc.cm officially ratifies the Cameroon-Korea visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders.
2026-06-27 - UPDATED transit visa max_stay_days from 4 to 5 days: diplocam.cm (MINREX official source) specifies 5-day maximum for the free transit visa.
2026-06-27 - UPDATED long-stay visa fee notes: diplocam.cm specifies XAF 150,000 normal / XAF 200,000 express for long-stay visas.
2026-06-27 - CONFIRMED Algeria (DZA) ratification: Decree No. 2026/034 of 29 January 2026 confirmed via prc.cm.
ADDED Gabon (GAB) to visa_free: CEMAC member confirmed via cameroontradehub.cm.
ADDED Equatorial Guinea (GNQ) to visa_free: CEMAC member confirmed via cameroontradehub.cm.
CORRECTED Mali (MLI) classification: confirmed as visa-free under Agreement on Free Movement of Persons and Goods per Embassy Japan.
REMOVED the grouped CEMAC bloc entry: Replaced with individually listed entries per country.
gaps: Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt (ambacamcaire.com) exemption page lists ONLY Nigeria, Republic of Congo, CAR, and Chad - does NOT list Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, or Mali. This creates a discrepancy with Embassy Japan (Mali confirmed) and cameroontradehub.cm (GNQ/GAB via CEMAC). GNQ, GAB, and MLI are KEPT in visa_free per safety rules and per CEMAC treaty confirmation (cameroontradehub.cm) and Embassy Japan confirmation (MLI).

CEMAC free movement biometric passport requirement: cameroontradehub.cm specifies biometric passport is required; it is unclear whether a non-biometric CEMAC national passport is rejected at border or simply unadvised. Recommend confirming with MINREX or DGSN.

Mali free movement agreement status: Wikipedia reports the exemption was cancelled September 2015 and later restored at unspecified date. No official decree date for restoration found on prc.cm or other official sources. Embassy Japan confirms current expense-free status.
Mali ordinary passport visa-free: Embassy Egypt does NOT list Mali. Embassy Japan confirms expense-free. Discrepancy unresolved - Mali kept as visa_free per Embassy Japan official confirmation.
Nigeria free movement for all nationals confirmed from Embassy Japan and Embassy Egypt pages but no treaty text found on official .gov.cm source.
Gabon ordinary passport visa-free status confirmed from cameroontradehub.cm (CEMAC treaty) - no direct ambacamcaire.com or cameroonemb-jp.org explicit listing for GAB ordinary passports.
Equatorial Guinea ordinary passport visa-free status confirmed from cameroontradehub.cm (CEMAC treaty) - no direct ambacamcaire.com or cameroonemb-jp.org explicit listing for GNQ ordinary passports.

Transit visa discrepancy: diplocam.cm (MINREX) says free transit up to 5 days; Cameroon High Commission UK says EUR 153 for transit 4-30 days. These may be separate products (free micro-transit vs. paid transit visa). Recommend clarification from MINREX.

Korea (KOR) conditional_access decree: prc.cm Decree No. 2025/176 confirmed but full text not extractable (PDF image only). Passport types (diplomatic/service) and 90-day max stay taken from prior Wikipedia/aggregator sources. Wikipedia (re-fetched 2026-06-29) incorrectly shows Korea as still awaiting ratification - prc.cm is authoritative.
Residence permit minimum investment thresholds for investors not published on official government sources.
Dual citizenship policy not explicitly stated on official government pages found.
Path to permanent residency or citizenship timelines not published on official pages found.
MINREX official website (minrex.gov.cm) DNS resolution error; diplocam.cm used as MINREX official source.

No third-country credential conditional access rules found on any official Cameroon government source: no provision for holders of US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, Canadian visa, Australian visa, residence permits, or OCI cards to enter Cameroon visa-free or on arrival.

Exact agreement dates for bilateral diplomatic/service passport waivers with Brazil, Switzerland, China, Italy, Russia, Turkey, UAE, Vietnam, Equatorial Guinea, and Mali not found on official government sources.
Egypt and Tunisia decree dates sourced from Cameroon Intelligence Report (not a .gov domain); official decree text on prc.cm not verified for each country.
UAE and Vietnam waiver scope (diplomatic only vs. diplomatic+service) confirmed from Embassy Japan, but not from a primary official treaty text.

sources_checked: https://ambacamcaire.com/consular-services/general-info/exemption-to-the-requirement-of-an-entry-visa/ - Official Embassy of Cameroon in Egypt - re-fetched 2026-06-29; lists Nigeria, Republic of Congo, CAR, Chad only (NOT Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Mali)

http://cameroonemb-jp.org/visa/info-visa-application/ - Official Cameroon Embassy in Japan - re-fetched 2026-06-29; confirmed Nigeria free movement + Mali expense-free visa for ordinary; bilateral exemption list for diplomatic/service (Tunisia listed as 'not yet in force')
https://www.evisacam.cm/ - Official MINREX e-visa portal - re-fetched 2026-06-29; login/registration interface only, no public visa exemption list

https://cameroonhighcommission.co.uk/faq-e-visa-application-procedures/ - Official Cameroon High Commission UK - re-fetched 2026-06-29; confirmed fees EUR 153/229/305; transit visa listed as EUR 153 for 4-30 days (discrepancy with diplocam.cm free 5-day transit noted)

https://www.diplocam.cm/e-visa/ - Official MINREX/diplocam.cm portal - re-fetched 2026-06-29; confirmed XAF 100,000/150,000 short-stay fees; XAF 150,000/200,000 long-stay fees; transit visa 5-day maximum (free)

https://www.cameroontradehub.cm/article/59/en - Official Cameroon Trade Hub - re-fetched 2026-06-29; confirms CEMAC free movement effective; references Cameroon Tribune N 11461 of 27 October 2017; names GNQ, GAB, COG, CAF, TCD, CMR as CEMAC states; biometric passport required

https://www.prc.cm/en/news/the-acts/decrees/7726-decree-no-2025-176-of-2-may-2025-to-ratify-the-agreement-on-reciprocal-exemption-from-visa-requirements-between-cameroon-and-korea - Official Cameroon Presidency (prc.cm) - confirmed Decree No. 2025/176 of 2 May 2025 ratifying Cameroon-Korea visa exemption for diplomatic/service passports

https://prc.cm/en/news/the-acts/decrees/8153-decree-no-2026-033-of-29-january-2026-to-ratify-the-bilateral-agreement-relating-to-air-transport-services-between-cameroon-and-qatar-2 - Official Cameroon Presidency (prc.cm) - re-fetched 2026-06-29; confirmed Decree No. 2026/034 of 29 January 2026 ratifying Cameroon-Algeria mutual visa exemption for diplomatic/service passport holders

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Cameroon - Wikipedia (aggregator) - re-fetched 2026-06-29; confirms 7 visa-free countries; shows Korea as 'awaiting ratification' (outdated per prc.cm); notes Mali cancellation in 2015 and unspecified restoration

https://prc.cm/en/news/the-acts/decrees/ - Official Cameroon Presidency decrees index - fetched 2026-06-29; most recent page covers judicial/financial decrees; no new visa-related decrees found beyond what already confirmed
